title: ECDH, X25519, and Authenticated Key Agreement · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Shared Secret Kdf
summary: A device protocol treats ECDH output as an encryption key, never authenticates peer keys, accepts malformed encodings, and reuses ephemeral keys indefinitely. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the shared secret kdf increment in authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Feed the ECDH result and full context into a labeled KDF rather than using raw shared bytes directly. A defensible solution applies Crypto Go Shared Secret Kdf, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript.

content.code:
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"crypto/ecdh"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	curve := ecdh.X25519()
	leftBytes, rightBytes := make([]byte, 32), make([]byte, 32)
	leftBytes[31], rightBytes[31] = 4, 5
	left, leftErr := curve.NewPrivateKey(leftBytes)
	right, rightErr := curve.NewPrivateKey(rightBytes)
	if leftErr != nil || rightErr != nil { panic("fixed fixture rejected") }
	leftSecret, errLeft := left.ECDH(right.PublicKey())
	rightSecret, errRight := right.ECDH(left.PublicKey())
	fmt.Printf("shared=%t errors=%t authenticated=false kdf_required=true\n", bytes.Equal(leftSecret, rightSecret), errLeft == nil && errRight == nil)
}
